dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for chest pain.
Hi, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
Uh, chest pain started about 30 minutes ago. I have a burning feeling on my left side.
You're alert and oriented to person, place, time, and event.
You're alert and oriented to person, place, time, and event (A/O x 4).
Patient is alert and oriented with a GCS of 15.
I have a 9 out of 10 constant burning in the left chest. It doesn't get better or worse with movement.
Chest rise is equal and to normal effort.
Breath sounds are clear and equal in both lungs.
Starting four-lead cardiac monitoring now.
CV checks: 4 lead ECG monitoring is initiated now.
12 lead ECG is being taken. Preliminary interpretation is sinus rhythm with nonspecific T wave anomalies.
No deformities, contusions, punctures, abrasions, or lacerations on the torso, arms, or legs.
No deformities, contusions, or punctures noted on upper and lower extremities.
Your abdomen is swollen and tender. Pain when I press here, ma'am?
Abdomen is distended with guarding in the lower left quadrant. No rebound or rigidity.
Pupil size, movement, sensation and coordination all normal and equal bilaterally.
PMS: Positive on both sides; no deficits in sensation or movement noted.
Coordination is normal on both sides.
All extremity strength is normal and equal.
Gait is unremarkable with no signs of instability.
Pupils equal, round, and reactive at 3 mm.
Skin is warm, dry, and has a normal color.
You mentioned chest pain this morning. Can you tell me about any other symptoms or changes you've noticed?
I usually wake up very tired. I noticed swelling in my feet, right hand, and my belly.
I understand you were awoken at 4 AM with chest discomfort and tried using the bathroom.
I took some laxatives last night and had a small move. This morning I couldn't go.
You currently take Lantus, Lotrel, Buspar, Dexilant, Cymbalta, Fosinopril, Remeron, Seroquel, Ranolazine, Crestor, Ambien, Valium, Proventil, Keflex.
Your medical history includes hypertension, diabetes, asthma, anxiety, and depression.
You have a known seafood allergy.
Heart rate is 100 beats per minute.
Respiratory rate is 16 breaths per minute.
Blood pressure is 95 over 61.
Oxygen saturation is 98%.
Initial vitals: pulse 100, respiratory 16, blood pressure 95/61, and saturation 98%.
Blood glucose was unavailable but later noted at 205.
Oxygen saturation has dropped to 96%.
EKG shows sinus rhythm with nonspecific T wave abnormality.
Current vitals: pulse 100, respirations 16, blood pressure 83 over 55, oxygen saturation 96%.
Rechecking vitals: pulse 95, respiration 16, blood pressure now 117 over 69, saturation at 95%.
Administered 324 mg of aspirin orally as per standing order.
IV started in the right arm with a 20 gauge catheter.
Placing you in the semi-Fowler's position for transport with shoulder and lap belts for safety.
Current vitals: Blood pressure is 117 over 69 and oxygen saturation at 95%.
Heart rate has decreased to 95.
EKG remains in sinus rhythm.
Transport is in progress under protocol 2-1 for suspected chest pain.
Pre-arrival report has been sent to [COMPANY_NAME] with your EPCR.
You are currently alert and stable. We are preparing to depart now.
Patient remains alert and oriented (A/O x 4).
Rechecking breath sounds: clear and equal in both lungs.
No new findings on torso, arms, or legs - DCAP-BTLS remains negative.
PMS remains positive bilaterally with normal movement and sensation.
Respirations remain 16 per minute and effort is normal.
Oxygen saturation at 95% stable for transport.
Vitals are stable during prep for transport.
Securing you in the semi-Fowler position with shoulder and lap belts for transport.
ASA PO 324 mg was given per protocol to help with suspected cardiac chest pain.
Ready for transport with patient in semi-Fowler.
Ready to depart with patient secured for transport.
ED room 13 at [COMPANY_NAME] is ready for your arrival with the pre-arrival report in place.
No complications expected with transport to [COMPANY_NAME], Cold.
EKG remains in sinus rhythm for the time being.
Final EKG interpretation: sinus rhythm with nonspecific T wave anomalies.
Transport destination confirmed as [COMPANY_NAME].
Victim securely placed in the stretcher for transport in semi-Fowler position with proper restraints.
We are transporting under protocol 2-1 with all standard procedures in place.
Recap: You have a history of hypertension, diabetes, asthma, anxiety, and depression, all contributing to your current presentation.
Continuous cardiac monitoring in place with no acute changes observed.
You are secure and stable for transport. Let's go.
EMS team standing by for departure to hospital now.
Transportation will proceed under medical chest pain protocol 2-1.
Calling hospital now to ensure ED room is prepared upon arrival.
All set for transport.
All set, we're rolling out to the hospital.
Rechecked: No DCAP-BTLS findings on torso and extremities.
PERRL examination confirms 3 mm bil laterally.
Strength analysis done on upper and lower limbs: normal and equal.
Gait testing confirms no abnormalities noted.
Final pulse reading of 95 per minute prior to transport.
Final saturation recorded prior to transport is 95%.
Final EKG shows sinus rhythm with no acute changes noted.
Patient states symptoms began after repeated strain to move bowels this morning with prior ligative use.
Known medical history: Hypertension, Type 2 diabetes, asthma, anxiety, and depression.
Destination confirmed for possible cardiac chest pain.
Cold weather transport to [COMPANY_NAME] should not affect stability.
Moving to ambulance with patient secured and vitals stable.
Transporting the patient with semi-reclined position secured.
IV remains patent during transport, fluids kept at maintenance level.
Transport is proceeding without incident.
Transporting under protocol 2-1 for suspected cardiac cause.
All data including IV access has been reported to ED.
Forget noting, we're moving to auscultate in the ambulance now.
All tools and equipment accounted for at destination.
All restraints secure in transit.
Aspirin was held prior to transport to monitor without medication effect.
Rechecking EKG on move, all findings consistent with prior.
Cold save in process in vehicle now.
Rechecking vitals in transient phase.
No new symptoms, staying on protocol 2-1.
